record.field: Forgejo is a lightweight, self-hosted software forge that gives you full control over your code, issues, and CI without depending on a commercial platform.

Forgejo is a lightweight, self-hosted software forge that gives you full control over your code, issues, and CI without depending on a commercial platform.

It provides a complete GitHub-like experience for Git hosting, code review, and project collaboration, packaged as a single Go binary that runs comfortably on modest hardware. A community fork of Gitea, Forgejo is governed by the non-profit Codeberg e.V. and is fully free software, with a strong emphasis on transparency, privacy, and long-term data portability. It stays highly compatible with Gitea at the database and API level, making migrations in either direction straightforward.

Key features
  • Git repository hosting with pull requests, issues, wikis, and code review
  • Built-in CI/CD via Forgejo Actions, compatible with GitHub Actions workflow syntax
  • Integrated container and package registries plus Git LFS support
  • Authentication via LDAP, OAuth2/OIDC, SAML, SMTP, and PAM
  • Built-in importers for GitHub, GitLab, Gitea, Gogs, and plain Git URLs
  • Low resource footprint as a single static binary, with emerging ActivityPub federation
Best for

Teams, organizations, and individuals who want a private, low-maintenance GitHub alternative with built-in CI/CD, especially those who value non-profit community governance, copyleft licensing, and full ownership of their development infrastructure.

services: forgejo: image: codeberg.org/forgejo/forgejo:10 environment: - USER_UID=${USER_UID} - USER_GID=${USER_GID} - FORGEJO__database__DB_TYPE=postgres - FORGEJO__database__HOST=db:5432 - FORGEJO__database__NAME=forgejo - FORGEJO__database__USER=forgejo - FORGEJO__database__PASSWD=forgejo restart: always volumes: - ./forgejo_server:/data - /etc/timezone:/etc/timezone:ro - /etc/localtime:/etc/localtime:ro depends_on: - db container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}' ports: - '{{ PORT_HTTP }}:3000' networks: - internal - agz-proxy-network db: image: postgres:17 restart: always environment: - POSTGRES_USER=forgejo - POSTGRES_PASSWORD=forgejo - POSTGRES_DB=forgejo volumes: - ./forgejo_db:/var/lib/postgresql/data container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}_db' networks: - internal networks: internal: driver: bridge agz-proxy-network: external: true

#!/bin/bash set -euo pipefail cd "$SERVICE_PATH" # Dump consistente de PostgreSQL (servicio en marcha) → $BACKUP_DATA_DIR docker compose exec -T db sh -c 'pg_dump -U "$POSTGRES_USER" -d "$POSTGRES_DB" --no-owner -Fc' \ > "$BACKUP_DATA_DIR/db.dump" echo "pg_dump OK ($(wc -c < "$BACKUP_DATA_DIR/db.dump") bytes)"

#!/bin/bash set -euo pipefail cd "$SERVICE_PATH" # Levanta solo la DB, espera readiness y restaura docker compose up -d db for i in $(seq 1 30); do docker compose exec -T db sh -c 'pg_isready -U "$POSTGRES_USER"' && break sleep 2 done docker compose exec -T db sh -c 'pg_restore -U "$POSTGRES_USER" -d "$POSTGRES_DB" --clean --if-exists --no-owner' \ < "$RESTORE_DATA_DIR/db.dump" # Verificación: la DB no quedó vacía (sh -c con dobles comillas; $VAR del contenedor con \$) N=$(docker compose exec -T db sh -c "psql -U \$POSTGRES_USER -d \$POSTGRES_DB -tAc \"SELECT count(*) FROM information_schema.tables WHERE table_schema='public'\"") echo "tablas restauradas: $N" test "$N" -gt 0
